beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to a sunny Monday; Ingrid rises early to catch the good light and plan her day around the weather.

beats.activity: Commute to pediatric dental practice in Kirkland.
beats.reasoning: Early drive to Kirkland before the morning rush; no transit delays affect this route.

beats.activity: Morning pediatric dental appointments and patient care.
beats.reasoning: Standard morning clinic hours; Ingrid sees patients before the noon break and doesn't prioritize the 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Lunch break; try a new seasonal spot near the practice.
beats.reasoning: Ingrid is an adventurous eater; she grabs a fresh, local lunch nearby rather than rushing home or watching the match.

beats.activity: Afternoon pediatric dental appointments and administrative work.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon clinic block; Ingrid finishes the work day with time to spare before evening.
Evening drive home; Light Rail closure doesn't affect car commute from Kirkland to Shoreline.

beats.activity: Water and tend to urban garden beds; photograph evening light on plants.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny evening to enjoy her garden and capture film photography; Ingrid prioritizes outdoor time while weather is ideal.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a light dinner.
beats.reasoning: Simple evening meal at home; Ingrid uses fresh vegetables from her garden.

diary: Monday started like most do—coffee in the quiet kitchen, watching the light come through the Shoreline house. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, thank goodness. The Light Rail closure didn't touch me this time, though I noticed the bars downtown were already buzzing about that USA-Wales match at eleven. I had the usual stream of kids in the chair, one little girl terrified of the water spray until I let her hold the suction tube herself. Changed everything. Lunch at The Herbalist was a nice break—their new seasonal salad actually had substance to it. Afternoon flew by with paperwork and more appointments. By evening, I just wanted to be outside. The light was perfect, so I spent time with my garden beds, took some photos of how the golden hour hit the tomato plants. Made a simple dinner and felt the day settle into me—tired but satisfied.
thought_process: I woke up thinking about the World Cup buzz but honestly, I'm not caught up in it this year. The sunny weather and that perfect early-June temperature lifted my mood more than sports news ever could. I chose The Herbalist for lunch because I needed something different, something that felt intentional rather than routine. The afternoon appointments were routine pediatric work, but that one girl's fear breaking when she took control—that reminded me why I still do this at nearly sixty. I wasn't tempted to detour downtown to catch the match with the crowds. Instead, I gravitated toward my garden beds. Photography and plants are my real passion now, more than they've been in years. The light at evening was just too good to waste.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
